questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Brown ring test is used for the detection of which radical?
questions.solution: Brown ring test is used for the detection of nitrate.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Give the correct order of initials T or F for following statements. Use T if statement is true and F if it is false.

(i) Cu+ undergoes disproportionation to Cu and Cu2+ in aqueous solution

(ii) Hg2Cl2 does not impart chromyl chloride test

(iii) Sulphide ions react with sodium nitroprusside to form a purple coloured complex. In this reaction, oxidation state of iron changes.
questions.solution:
(i)   2 Cu + aq → Cu 2 + aq + Cu s

(ii)   Hg2Cl2 does not furnish Cl- , for chromyl chloride test.

(iii)   Na 2 S + Na 2 Fe CN 5 NO → Violet colour
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A red solid is insoluble in water. However, it becomes soluble if some  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></math>  is added to water. Heating the red solid in a test tube results in liberation of some violet coloured fumes and droplets of a metal appear on the cooler parts of the test tube. The red solid is
questions.solution: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>  is orange-red in appearance and hardly soluble in water. It dissolves in excess of  <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math>   giving complexes  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msubsup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math>  and  <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msubsup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">I</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> . Violet fumes are liberated due to the release of Iodine gas upon heating.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A mixture of two salts is not water soluble but dissolves completely in dilute <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCI</mi></math> to form a colourless solution. The mixture could be:
questions.solution: BaCO3+ZnS mixture dissolves in HCl but is insoluble in water. Further, the solution in HCl will be colourless due to the formation of soluble BaCl2 and ZnCl2.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Passing <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> in to a mixture of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions in an acidified aqueous solution precipitates
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> ad <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>
questions.solution: <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> in presence of aqueous solution precipitates sulphide of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> (IInd group) apart from <math><mtext>P</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>b</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mtext>B</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>i</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mtext>C</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>d</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mtext>A</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>s</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mtext>S</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>b</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> and <math><mtext>S</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>n</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mtext>M</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>n</mtext></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mtext>N</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>i</mtext></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math>  are of IVth  group so cannot be precipitated by <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
X → KOH Y (gas turns red litmus blue) + Z → Zn + KOH Y (gas) .

X → Δ gas (does not support combustion)

Identify (X) to (Z):
questions.solution:
NH 4 NO 2 X + KOH → NH 3 ↑ Y + KNO 2 Z + H 2 O

NO 2 - Z + 3 Zn + 5 OH - + 5 H 2 O → 3 Zn OH 4 2 - soluble + NH 3 ↑ Y

NH 4 NO 2 → Δ N 2 ↑ + 2 H 2 O X (Does not support combustion)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A chemistry student trying to detect the metallic ion in a salt, makes a paste on a clean platinum wire loop of the salt with concentrated <math><mtext>H</mtext><mtext>C</mtext><mtext>l</mtext></math> . When he takes a small amount of this paste and keeps it in a non-luminous Bunsen flame, the colour of the flame changes to grassy green. He should, therefore, conclude that the metal is
questions.solution: <math><mtext>′</mtext><mtext>B</mtext><mtext>a</mtext><mtext>′</mtext></math> gives characteristic green flame in Bunsen burner.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In what order the reagents <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math> , <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math> are added to an aqueous solution containing  <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mtext>Ag</mtext></mrow> <mo>+</mo></msup><mo>,</mo><msup> <mrow> <mtext>Cu</mtext></mrow> <mrow> <mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math> and <math> <mrow> <msup> <mrow> <mtext>Ni</mtext></mrow> <mrow> <mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math> ions in order to precipitate <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> first <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">u</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> second and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math> last.
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>
questions.solution:
With NaCl only Ag+ precipitates Ag++NaCl↓+Na+
With Nal,Cu+2 precipitates 2Cu+2+4I-→Cu2I2↓+I2
With Na2S,Ni+2 precipitates Ni+2+Na2S→NiS↓+2Na+
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Gradual addition of potassium iodide solution to aqueous <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><msub><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> solution initially produces a dark brown precipitate which dissolves in excess of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">KI</mi></math> to give a clear yellow solution. Identify the yellow precipitate.
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>
questions.solution:
At first BiNO33 undergoes hydrolysis.

Nitric acid is formed which oxidises I − to iodine. The liberated iodine dissolves in K to form yellow precipitate of KI3 .

BiNO33+ H2O→BiOHNO32 + HNO3

2HNO3→H2O + 2NO2+ O

2KI+ H2O + O→2KOH +I2Dark brown

I2+ KI→KI3yellow solution
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The correct option(s) to distinguish nitrate salts of Mn2+ and Cu2+ taken separately is (are)

(1) Mn2+ shows the characteristic green colour in the flame test

(2) only Cu2+ shows the formation of precipitate by passing H2S in acidic medium

(3) only Mn2+ shows the formation of precipitate by passing H2S in faintly basic medium

(4) Cu 2+ |Cu has higher reduction potential than Mn 2+ |Mn (measured under similar conditions)
questions.solution:
A Manganese show pale purple colour in flame test.

B  Cu2+→ H2SHCl CuS↓Black ppt.

C Both Cu2+ and Mn2+ form precipitate with H2S in basic medium.

D E Cu 2+ |Cu o =+0.34V, E Mn 2+ |Mn o =−1.18V
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math><mtext>P</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>b</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>2</mo><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> <math><mtext>C</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>u</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>2</mo><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo></math> <math><mtext>Z</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>n</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>2</mo><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> and <math><mtext>N</mtext><msup><mrow><mtext>i</mtext></mrow><mrow><mo>2</mo><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions are present in a given acidic solution. On passing hydrogen sulphide gas through this solution, the available precipitate will contain
questions.solution: Pb<sup>2+</sup> and Cu<sup>2+</sup> are precipitated as sulphides because of their lower solubility products, where as sulphide of other metals remain in the solution because of their high solubility products.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Pick out the incorrect statement.
options: Mg also burns in gases such as <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>
Excess of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> when passed in lime-water turns it milky

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>.</mo><mn>6</mn><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> on heating hydrolyses to form MgO (magnesia), which is refractory
Alkaline-earth metals are denser and harder than alkali metals
questions.solution:
Here statement in option B is incorrect as when CO2 is passed in lime-water, it becomes milky due to the formation of CaCO3 but excess of CO2 leads to the formation of clear solution, due to the formation of soluble Ca(HCO3)2 .

A. Mg+CO2→2MgO+C
and 2Mg+SO2→2MgO+S

D. Inter-atomic forces (or metallic bonding) in alkaline-earth metals are stronger than in alkali metals.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
In the following reaction sequence in aqueous solution, the species X, Y and Z respectively, are

S 2 O 3 2− → Ag + X    Clear  Solution → Ag + Y     White Precipitate → With time Z      Black Precipitate
options: <math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msub><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>

<math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msub><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>

<math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msub><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math>

<math><msup><mrow><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><msub><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">gS</mi></math>
questions.solution: So, X,Y and Z are AgS2O323-, Ag2S2O3 and Ag2S respectively.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: How do you characterizes <math><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math> ?
options: It is soluble in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math>

It is insoluble in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math>
questions.solution:
PbCrO4 is yellow solution soluble in NaOH
PbCrO4+NaOH→No2PbOH4+Na2CrO4

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A solution of a metal ion when treated with KI gives a red precipitate which dissolves in excess KI to give a colourless solution. Moreover, the solution of metal ion on treatment with a solution of cobalt (II) thiocyanate gives rise to a deep blue crystalline precipitate. The metal ion is
questions.solution:
When a solution of a Hg2+ is treated with KI a red precipitate of (HgI2) is obtained which dissolves in excess KI to give a colourless solution (HgI4)2- . When the solution of Hg2+ is treated with a solution of cobalt (II) thiocyanate a deep blue crystalline precipitate of Hg(SCN)2 is formed. The reactions are as follows
Hg2++2I–→HgI2
HgI2(excess)+2I–→[HgI4]2–Soluble
Hg 2+ +Co (SCN) 2 → Hg (SCN) 2 Blue crystal line ppt.

Here other ion Pb2+ , Hg2+ and Co2+ do not give precipitates formed in these reactions.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.solution: Magnesium donot give flame test in Bunsen burner flame.

questions.q: A salt of <math> <mrow> <mtext>NaX</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mover> <mo>→</mo> <mrow> <msub> <mrow> <mtext>MgCl</mtext></mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msub> </mrow> </mover> </mrow> </math> white ppt. on boiling. Thus, anion <math><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> is:
questions.solution: Sodium bicarbonate with magnesium chloride solution gives magnesium bicarbonate which is water soluble. On heating bicarbonate decomposes to carbonate, white ppt.

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: Neutral ferric chloride is added to the aqueous solution of acetate. The blood red colour is obtained, it is due to the compound
questions.solution: <math> <mrow> <mn>3</mn><mtext>C</mtext><msub> <mtext>H</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> <mtext>C</mtext><mtext>O</mtext><mtext>O</mtext><mtext>N</mtext><mtext>a</mtext><mo>+</mo><munder> <mrow> <mtext>F</mtext><mtext>e</mtext><mtext>C</mtext><msub> <mtext>l</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> </mrow> <mrow> <mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mtext>N</mtext><mtext>e</mtext><mtext>u</mtext><mtext>t</mtext><mtext>r</mtext><mtext>a</mtext><mtext>l</mtext><mo stretchy="false">)</mo> </mrow> </munder> <mo>→</mo><msup> <mrow> <munder> <mrow> <mo stretchy="false">[</mo><mtext>F</mtext><msub> <mtext>e</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> <msub> <mrow> <mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mtext>O</mtext><mtext>H</mtext><mo stretchy="false">)</mo> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msub> <mrow> <mo stretchy="false">(</mo><mtext>C</mtext><msub> <mtext>H</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> <mtext>C</mtext><mtext>O</mtext><mtext>O</mtext><mo stretchy="false">)</mo> </mrow> <mn>6</mn> </msub> <mo stretchy="false">]</mo> </mrow> <mrow> <mtext>B</mtext><mtext>l</mtext><mtext>o</mtext><mtext>o</mtext><mtext>d</mtext><mtext> </mtext><mtext>r</mtext><mtext>e</mtext><mtext>d</mtext> </mrow> </munder> </mrow> <mo>+</mo> </msup> <mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mtext>N</mtext><mtext>a</mtext><mtext>C</mtext><mtext>l</mtext> </mrow> </math>

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The metal that produces red violet colour in the non-luminous flame is
questions.solution: Alkali metals and their salts impart a characteristic colour to flame. The reason for flame colouration is that the energy of the flame causes an excitation of the outermost electrons which on return to their original position give out the energy so absorbed in the visible region. So Rb gives a red-violet colour to the flame. Zn, Ag, Pb don’t show flame test as they have higher ionization energy so flame test is not possible.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Metal chloride A is soluble in hot water but insoluble in cold water. Select correct statement about A. Thus
options: A can give yellow ppt. with  <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">r</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math>

A can give white ppt with  <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math>
A can give yellow ppt with KI
All of the above are correct statements.
questions.solution:
PbCl2 is soluble in hot water but forms white needle like crystals on cooling.

PbCl 2 → K 2 CrO 4 PbCrO 4 ↓ Yellow ppt

PbCl 2 → K 2 SO 4 PbSO 4 ↓ White ppt

PbCl 2 → KI PbI 2 ↓ Yellow ppt
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: A doctor by mistake administers a Ba(NO<sub>3</sub>)<sub>2</sub> solution to a patient for radiography investigations. Which of the following should be given as the best to prevent the absorption of soluble barium ?
questions.solution: <math><mrow> <mtext>Ba</mtext> <msub> <mrow> <mfenced close=")" open="(" separators=","> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>NO</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub> </mrow> </mfenced> </mrow> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <mo>+</mo> <msub> <mtext>Na</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msub> <mtext>SO</mtext> <mn>4</mn> </msub> <mo>⟶</mo> <munder> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>BaSO</mtext> <mn>4</mn> </msub> <mo>↓</mo> </mrow> <mtext>white ppt.</mtext> </munder> <mo>+</mo> <mn>2</mn> <msub> <mtext>NaNO</mtext> <mn>3</mn> </msub></mrow></math>
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Microcosmic salt bead test of salt <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> yields semi-translucent mass. Therefore it contains
questions.solution:
Silica is not dissolved by the phosphate bead. When a silicate salt is strongly heated in the bead, silica is liberated and this remain suspended in the bead in the form of semi-translucent mass.

CaSiO3+NaPO3→ NaCaPO4+SiO2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The colour of <math><mi>C</mi><mi>u</mi><mi>C</mi><msub><mrow><mi>r</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>7</mn></mrow></msub></math> solution in water is green because
options: <math><mi>C</mi><msup><mrow><mi>u</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions are blue and <math><mi>C</mi><msub><mrow><mi>r</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msubsup><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>7</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> ions are orange
questions.solution: <math><mi>C</mi><msup><mrow><mi>u</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>+</mo><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions are blue and <math><mi>C</mi><msub><mrow><mi>r</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><msubsup><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>7</mn></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mo>-</mo></mrow></msubsup></math> ions are orange, orange and blue combination gives-green colour.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The carbonate of which of the following cation is insoluble in water?
questions.solution:
Carbonates of alkali metal and ammonium are soluble in water.
All other carbonates are insoluble in water.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: A solution of metal hydroxide (MOH) with copper sulphate and mixed tartarate of metal M with another metal M<sub>1</sub> of the same group, is used in the detection of -CHO group. Metal M and M<sub>1</sub> are respectively :
questions.solution: NaOH + CuSO<sub>4</sub> + Sodium (M) + Potassium (M<sub>1</sub>) tartarate solution is known as Fehling's solution and is used in the detection of -CHO group.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following isomers will give white precipitate with <math><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> solution?
questions.solution:
White precipitate is due to formation of barium sulphate.
[Co(NH3)5Br]SO4⇌[Co(NH3)5Br]2++SO42-
SO 4 2−  +  BaCl 2 → BaSO 4 white ppt  +  2Cl −
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which metal will give blue colour metaborate in borax bead test?
questions.solution: In borax bead test copper gives blue coloured copper metaborate.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following acid radical will not produce a precipitate with <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></math> solution?
questions.solution: With <math><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo></mrow></msup></math> , no precipitate is obtained because of low lattice energy of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></math>, so it remains in ionized state.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
